content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN intervals [L1,R1],[L2,R2],…,[LN,RN][L_1, R_1], [L_2, R_2], \ldots, [L_N, R_N][L1​,R1​],[L2​,R2​],…,[LN​,RN​]. Consider NNN integers x1,x2,…,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1​,x2​,…,xN​ such that:


xi∈[Li,Ri]x_i \in [L_i, R_i]xi​∈[Li​,Ri​] for each valid iii
all decimal digits of S=x1+x2+x3+…+xNS = x_1 + x_2 + x_3 + \ldots + x_NS=x1​+x2​+x3​+…+xN​ (without leading zeroes) lie between AAA and BBB inclusive
In how many different ways can you pick the sequence x1,x2,…,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1​,x2​,…,xN​? Since this number may be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: 0 ≤ Li ≤ Ri < 10^9 for each valid i
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N, A and B. N lines follow. For each i (1 ≤ i ≤ N), the i-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers Li and Ri.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of ways to choose x1, x2, …, xN, modulo 10^9 + 7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are 6 ways to choose x1 and x2 such that their sum is between 2 and 4 (inclusive) and all decimal digits of the sum are between 2 and 4 (inclusive). The possible sums are 2, 3, and 4.

There are 10 ways to choose x1, x2, and x3 such that their sum is between 1 and 3 (inclusive) and all decimal digits of the sum are between 1 and 3 (inclusive). The possible sums are 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to calculate the number of ways to choose x1, x2, …, xN. We will create a DP table where dp[i][j] represents the number of ways to choose the first i numbers such that their sum is j. We will iterate over all possible values of i and j, and for each i, we will iterate over all possible values of Li and Ri. We will update the DP table accordingly and finally return the value of dp[N][S] modulo 10^9 + 7.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * 10^9) — because we are iterating over all possible values of i, j, Li, and Ri.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * 10^9) — because we need to store the DP table.
